title: Health & time JSON API
spec: Write a single Node.js file `server.js` using ONLY the built-in `http` module (no external dependencies). It must serve: GET /health -> JSON {"status":"ok","uptime": <process uptime in seconds, number>}; GET /time -> JSON {"now": <current ISO 8601 timestamp string>}; any other path -> 404 JSON {"error":"not found"}. Listen on port 3000. Set Content-Type application/json on all responses.

title: In-memory Todo REST API
spec: Write a single Node.js file `server.js` (built-in `http` module only, no external deps) implementing an in-memory Todo REST API on port 3000. Endpoints: GET /todos -> array of todos; POST /todos with JSON body {"title":string} -> create todo {id, title, done:false}, 201; GET /todos/:id -> one todo or 404; PUT /todos/:id with JSON body {title?, done?} -> update, 404 if missing; DELETE /todos/:id -> 204, 404 if missing. Validate input (reject missing/empty title with 400). Use correct HTTP status codes and JSON responses.

title: Token-bucket rate limiter
spec: Write a single Node.js file `limiter.js` (built-in modules only) implementing a token-bucket rate limiter as HTTP middleware usable with the built-in `http` server. Per-client-IP buckets with configurable capacity and refill rate (tokens per second). When a request has no token available, respond 429 with a `Retry-After` header (seconds until next token) and JSON {"error":"rate limited"}. Export the middleware factory. Include a runnable self-check (in `if (require.main === module)`) that simulates rapid requests from an IP and asserts the limiter allows the capacity then blocks, and refills over time.

title: Responsive pricing section
spec: Write a single self-contained `index.html` (inline CSS, no external resources or frameworks) containing a responsive pricing section with exactly 3 plan cards: Starter ($9/mo), Pro ($29/mo), Enterprise ($99/mo). Each card shows the plan name, price, a list of 4 features, and a call-to-action button. Visually highlight the middle (Pro) card as the recommended plan. Layout must be responsive: 3 columns on desktop, stacking to 1 column on narrow screens.

title: Todo app with localStorage
spec: Write a single self-contained `index.html` (inline CSS + vanilla JS, no frameworks or external resources) implementing a Todo app. Features: input to add a todo; each item can be toggled complete (with visual strike-through) and deleted; filter buttons All / Active / Completed; a counter showing remaining active items; persist all todos to localStorage so they survive reload. Provide a clean, usable UI.

title: Sales dashboard (no chart lib)
spec: Write a single self-contained `index.html` (inline CSS + vanilla JS, NO charting library, no external resources). Embed this dataset in the script: [{"month":"Jan","revenue":42000,"users":1200},{"month":"Feb","revenue":38500,"users":1100},{"month":"Mar","revenue":51200,"users":1450},{"month":"Apr","revenue":47800,"users":1380},{"month":"May","revenue":61000,"users":1720},{"month":"Jun","revenue":58300,"users":1650}]. Render: (1) a bar chart of monthly revenue, (2) a line chart of monthly users, (3) three KPI stat cards: total revenue, average monthly users, and best month by revenue. Draw the charts yourself with SVG or canvas. Make it look like a clean dashboard.

title: slugify()
spec: Write a Python file `slugify.py` with a function `slugify(text: str) -> str` that converts a string into a URL slug: lowercase; spaces and underscores become single hyphens; remove characters that are not a-z, 0-9, or hyphen; collapse consecutive hyphens into one; strip leading/trailing hyphens. Example: "  Hello, World!  " -> "hello-world". Include assert-based self-checks that run under `if __name__ == '__main__':` and cover normal, punctuation, and edge cases (empty string, all-symbols). Running `python slugify.py` must exit 0 when correct.

title: O(1) LRU cache
spec: Write a Python file `lru.py` implementing a class `LRUCache(capacity: int)` with `get(key)` (returns value or None) and `put(key, value)`, both O(1), evicting the least-recently-used item when capacity is exceeded. A `get` or `put` counts as a use. Include assert-based self-checks under `if __name__ == '__main__':` covering eviction order, updates to existing keys, and capacity limits. `python lru.py` must exit 0 when correct.

title: Expression evaluator (no eval)
spec: Write a Python file `calc.py` with a function `evaluate(expr: str) -> float` that parses and evaluates arithmetic expressions containing +, -, *, /, parentheses, and integer/decimal numbers, honoring standard operator precedence and associativity. Do NOT use eval() or exec(). Handle whitespace. Raise a clear exception on malformed input or division by zero. Include assert-based self-checks under `if __name__ == '__main__':` covering precedence, parentheses, decimals, and error cases. `python calc.py` must exit 0 when correct.

title: CSV→JSON CLI
spec: Write a Python CLI `csv2json.py` (stdlib only) that converts CSV to JSON. Usage: `python csv2json.py [file.csv]` prints a JSON array of objects (one per row, keyed by header). If no file argument is given, read CSV from stdin. Support a `--pretty` flag for indented output. Handle a missing file with a clear error message and non-zero exit. Include a self-check under `if __name__ == '__main__':` is NOT required to run by default (argparse drives main), but include a `--selfcheck` flag that runs assert-based tests on a small in-memory CSV and exits 0 on success.

title: Sales aggregation toolkit
spec: Write a Python file `sales.py` (stdlib only) operating on a list of sales records, each a dict like {"date":"2026-01-15","product":"Widget","amount": 19.99}. Provide functions: total_revenue(records) -> float; revenue_by_product(records) -> dict product->total; top_products(records, n) -> list of (product, total) sorted descending; monthly_revenue(records) -> dict 'YYYY-MM'->total. Include assert-based self-checks under `if __name__ == '__main__':` with a small sample dataset. `python sales.py` must exit 0 when correct.

title: Refactor over-engineered code
